meta.join_rule: For each book: numeric chapter N close paired with numeric chapter N+1 open. Plus epilogue2 close → epilogue3 open where both labelings have those entries (crystal_shard, halflings_gem). Excluded: prelude opens (no preceding close), book-part opens (streams_of_silver part1/part2/part3 — no preceding close in P14), epilogue opens for all books (no matching epilogue close in P14).

directional_verdict: Salvatore's chapter-seam transitions are dominated by the strong opener marginal (scene-set-description ~47% of opens). Only one transition appears in top-3 of >=2 books: partial-resolution -> scene-set-description (cs+sos top-1, hg falls outside top-3). Cells with non-zero count in ALL 3 books (stable positive rules, top 3): partial-resolution -> scene-set-description (n=17; cs=11/sos=4/hg=2); partial-resolution -> in-media-res-action (n=5; cs=2/sos=1/hg=2); cliffhanger -> scene-set-description (n=5; cs=2/sos=1/hg=2). Stable negative rules (zero across all 3 books with expected >= 1): foreshadow -> in-media-res-action (exp~2.33, obs=0); partial-resolution -> time-cut-announcement (exp~1.86, obs=0); foreshadow -> dialogue-first (exp~1.67, obs=0). The strongest independence-outlier (foreshadow -> time-cut-announcement, ratio 3.6) is crystal_shard-only (3/0/0) and does NOT generalize. The only directional pull that is both spread across all 3 books AND shows positive lift over the opener marginal is cliffhanger -> in-media-res-action (26.7% conditional vs 17.9% marginal; cs=1, sos=1, hg=2).
